name: File Transfer
description: Ingest files from SFTP, FTP, HTTP, local filesystem, or cloud storage (S3, GCS, Azure Blob). Protocol-discriminated configuration with entity-to-file mapping.
use_cases: HTTP file download (CSV, JSON, XML feeds)
Data lake ingestion from file drops

entity.name: File Transfer
entity.description: Protocol-agnostic file ingestion: SFTP, FTP, HTTP, S3, GCS, Azure Blob, or local filesystem.

transport.help: Protocol-specific transport configuration. Set transport.protocol to one of: sftp, ftp, ftps, http, https, file, s3, gs, az-blob.

authentication.help: Authentication configuration. Set authType appropriate for the transport protocol.

fileSpecs.help: Map of entity name → file location and parser config. Keys become sync entities.

errorHandling.help: Retry strategy and limits (retryStrategy, maxRetries, initialDelayMs, maxDelayMs).

connection_config_spec.help: Configuration is a structured JSON object with transport (discriminated union on protocol), authentication, fileSpecs, errorHandling, and timeout sections.

connector_package.description: Multi-protocol file ingestion connector. Runtime implemented as platform-native provider (FileTransferConnector). Supports S3 natively; SFTP/FTP/HTTP require sync-processor runtime for full file download/parse.
follow_up_notes: Runtime: FileTransferConnector in providers/file-transfer/ — uses platform_native protocol.
S3 operations (testConnection, listFiles, previewFile, fetchData) work immediately via AWS SDK.
SFTP/FTP operations require ssh2/basic-ftp packages bundled in sync-processor Lambda.
HTTP file download works for testConnection (HEAD); full fetch requires sync-processor.
Workflow integration: maps to api_poll graph node with LeoCron schedule.
